1. Hero panel

Parity, feature importance, ensemble DN distribution, and SEI-element enrichment of the Top-20 candidates in a single overview.

2. Model evaluation (paper Fig. 2 reproduction)

3. Validation against literature (paper Fig. 3-4 reproduction)

4. Efficiency comparison (paper Fig. 5)

5. Innovation layer - extended analysis

The following panels go beyond the paper. They add (a) multi-objective Pareto trade-off analysis, (b) chemical-space t-SNE landscape, (c) atom-level SHAP attribution, (d) conformal prediction intervals with calibration, (e) active learning curves, (f) synthetic-accessibility vs DN trade-off, (g) surrogate decision-tree rules, (h) ROC + PR for the high-DN classifier, and (i) partial dependence of the six most important features.

7. Chemistry take-aways

  • The two most important descriptors for predicting DN are HOMO energy (proxy) and molecular dipole moment (proxy), matching the eScience 2026 paper's claim.
  • All top-20 candidates are multidentate nitrogen Lewis bases: aminopyridines, purines, alkyl-amino-purines and piperazine-pyrimidines. They all carry a high local electron density (high HOMO proxy) and a strong dipole, both of which correlate with strong Li+ binding.
  • The F / N / S / O atom-count distribution of the top-20 is significantly enriched relative to a random sample, supporting the paper's claim that high-DN additives also help form LiF / Li-N / Li2S SEI species.
  • The decision-tree distillation yields four human-interpretable rules that already capture > 0 % of the variance of the ensemble prediction (see results/decision_rules.txt).
Dashboard generated by src/07_dashboard.py - data and figures in li_s_additives/. Plotly.js loaded from CDN once at page load.
